qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Unbounded Stack Growth in Pickle Interpreter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: Resource Exhaustion via Uncontrolled Stack Consumption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unbounded stack popping in fickling/fickle.py opcode handlers, while the advisory concerns the UnsafeImports blocklist missing cProfile in fickling/analysis.py.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZipSlip Path Traversal via Malicious ZIP Archive Extraction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unsanitized ZIP entry extraction in fickling/polyglot.py, while the advisory concerns the UnsafeImports blocklist missing cProfile in fickling/analysis.py.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Log Injection via Unsensitized Pickle Content in Tracing Output
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unsanitized trace output in fickling/tracing.py, while the advisory concerns the UnsafeImports blocklist missing cProfile in fickling/analysis.py.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described cProfile blocklist bypass in fickling/analysis.py::UnsafeImports or an equivalent source-to-sink/control-flow condition. The qualifying findings are unrelated open-world issues in stack handling, ZIP extraction, and trace output.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-p523-jq9w-64x9 describes Fickling <=0.1.6 classifying pickles using cProfile.run or cProfile.runctx as SUSPICIOUS because cProfile is missing from the UnsafeImports blocklist in fickling/analysis.py.
dos_unbounded_stack reports unbounded stack consumption in fickling/fickle.py opcode handlers such as AddItems.run, Dict.run, List.run, FrozenSet.run, and PopMark.run.
zipslip_path_traversal reports unsanitized ZIP archive extraction in fickling/polyglot.py:187.
log_injection_pickle_content reports unsanitized attacker-controlled content printed by Trace methods in fickling/tracing.py.
None of the qualifying reports describes cProfile, cProfile.run, cProfile.runctx, _lsprof, UnsafeImports, or the advisory's safety classification bypass from SUSPICIOUS instead of OVERTLY_MALICIOUS.
